title: Double Piston Extender
description: Pushes a single block two spaces out instead of one. A sticky piston faces the push direction; a second sticky piston sits behind it. When powered, the rear piston pushes the front piston forward, then the front piston extends — moving the head block two blocks total. Timing matters: the front piston must fire before the rear retracts. Built with a short repeater delay (2 ticks) on the rear piston's power line so the sequence is ordered correctly. Footprint is a 4-long line of blocks plus the redstone control line.
